member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.BackgroundWorkers.TokenCleanupWorker
member.summary: Background worker that periodically removes expired tokens from storage. Prevents unlimited growth of the database with stale token records. Typically runs once per hour but can be configured via dependency injection.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Caching.ICacheService
member.summary: Abstraction for caching layer to enable swapping implementations. Supports both simple memory caching and distributed cache backends. Essential for performance when validating frequently-accessed scopes, clients, and authorization codes.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Caching.ICacheService.GetAsync``1(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Retrieves a cached value by key. Returns null if key does not exist or has expired.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Caching.ICacheService.SetAsync``1(System.String,``0,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Stores a value in cache with optional expiration time.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Caching.ICacheService.RemoveAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Removes a cached value by key. Idempotent - does not throw if key does not exist.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Caching.ICacheService.RemoveByPatternAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Removes all cached values matching a pattern. Useful for invalidating related cache entries. Implementation depends on backend capabilities.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Caching.ICacheService.ClearAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Clears all cached values. Use sparingly - should only be called on graceful shutdown or explicit flush.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Caching.ICacheService.GetOrSetAsync``1(System.String,System.Func{System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{``0}},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets a value from cache or executes a factory function if not cached. Atomic operation - factory is only called once even in concurrent scenarios.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Caching.MemoryCacheService
member.summary: In-memory cache implementation using ConcurrentDictionary. Suitable for single-server deployments. For multi-server scenarios, consider using a distributed cache like Redis via dependency injection. Features automatic expiration checking and thread-safe operations.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Configuration.OpaOptions
member.summary: Options for the optional Open Policy Agent (OPA) integration. When is true the delegates policy decisions to the OPA REST API instead of evaluating them locally, allowing teams to manage and version policies externally using Rego.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Configuration.OpaOptions.Enabled
member.summary: Enables OPA-backed policy evaluation. When false (default) the built-in evaluator is used.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Configuration.OpaOptions.BaseUrl
member.summary: Base URL of the OPA server, e.g. "http://opa:8181".

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Configuration.OpaOptions.PolicyPath
member.summary: OPA policy path prefix, e.g. "authz". The full query URL becomes {BaseUrl}/v1/data/{PolicyPath}/{policyName}.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Configuration.OpaOptions.TimeoutSeconds
member.summary: HTTP request timeout in seconds when calling OPA.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Configuration.OpaOptions.FailClosedOnError
member.summary: When true, a failed or unreachable OPA call causes the policy evaluation to return false (deny). When false, a failure falls back to the built-in evaluator so normal operation continues if OPA is temporarily unavailable.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Controllers.ClientRegistrationController
member.summary: RFC 7591 Dynamic Client Registration endpoint. Allows OAuth2/OIDC clients to register themselves programmatically.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Controllers.ClientRegistrationController.RegisterClientAsync(DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Models.ClientRegistrationRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Register a new OAuth2 client (RFC 7591).
member.remarks:
POST /register with a JSON body containing client metadata. Returns 201 Created with the registered metadata and, for confidential clients, a generated client_secret.
Supported grant_types: authorization_code, client_credentials, implicit, refresh_token.
Set token_endpoint_auth_method to "none" for public clients (SPAs, native apps). Omit it or use "client_secret_basic" / "client_secret_post" for confidential clients.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential
member.summary: Stores the TOTP (RFC 6238) secret and state for a single user's MFA enrollment. The secret is a 20-byte random value encoded as Base32 (as expected by authenticator apps). Each user may have at most one active TOTP credential.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.Id
member.summary: Internal record identifier.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.UserId
member.summary: User that owns this credential.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.SecretKey
member.summary: Base32-encoded TOTP shared secret (RFC 4648 alphabet, no padding). Store this value encrypted at rest in production.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.IsEnabled
member.summary: Whether MFA has been confirmed and is actively enforced for this user. Setup is two-phase: the secret is generated first, then confirmed with a valid code.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.CreatedAt
member.summary: Timestamp when the credential record was first created (UTC).

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.EnabledAt
member.summary: Timestamp when MFA was confirmed and enabled (UTC).

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.LastUsedAt
member.summary: Timestamp of the last successful TOTP verification (UTC).

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.BackupCodes
member.summary: One-time backup codes that can each be used once in place of a TOTP code. Stored as hashes in production; plain for this in-memory demo.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.Enable
member.summary: Marks the credential as enabled after a successful code verification.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.TotpCredential.RecordVerification
member.summary: Records a successful TOTP verification.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential
member.summary: Represents a stored WebAuthn/FIDO2 public-key credential associated with a user account, enabling passwordless and phishing-resistant authentication via platform authenticators (e.g., Touch ID, Windows Hello) or roaming hardware security keys (e.g., YubiKey).

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.Id
member.summary: Gets or sets the internal unique identifier for this credential record.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.CredentialId
member.summary: Gets or sets the credential ID issued by the authenticator, stored as a Base64URL-encoded string. This identifier is presented by the client during each authentication ceremony.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.PublicKey
member.summary: Gets or sets the packed public key bytes extracted from the attestation response. For ES256, contains the P-256 x and y coordinates; for RS256, the modulus and exponent. See to determine the encoding.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.Algorithm
member.summary: Gets or sets the COSE algorithm identifier for the public key. Common values: -7 (ES256/P-256), -257 (RS256).

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.SignatureCounter
member.summary: Gets or sets the signature counter value from the most recent successful authentication. A received counter value lower than the stored value signals a potentially cloned authenticator.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.UserId
member.summary: Gets or sets the subject identifier (user ID) that owns this credential.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.FriendlyName
member.summary: Gets or sets a user-facing label for the credential (e.g., "MacBook Touch ID" or "YubiKey 5C").

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.AaGuid
member.summary: Gets or sets the AAGUID of the authenticator model as reported during attestation, formatted as a hyphenated GUID string (e.g., "adce0002-35bc-c60a-648b-0b25f1f05503").

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.BackupEligible
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this credential is eligible for cloud backup (the BE flag in authenticatorData). When , the credential may be a synced passkey.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.BackedUp
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the credential is currently backed up to a cloud service (the BS flag in authenticatorData). Meaningful only when is .

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.CreatedAt
member.summary: Gets or sets the UTC timestamp at which this credential was registered.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.LastUsedAt
member.summary: Gets or sets the UTC timestamp of the last successful authentication using this credential.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Entities.WebAuthnCredential.IsActive
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this credential is currently active. Setting to disables the credential without deleting the record.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Events.EventPublisher
member.summary: In-process event publisher for synchronous event distribution. Maintains a registry of subscribers and notifies them when matching events occur. Suitable for single-server deployments. For distributed systems, consider using a message broker like RabbitMQ or Kafka.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Events.EventPublisher.Subscribe``1(DotnetAuthServer.Events.IEventSubscriber{``0})
member.summary: Registers a subscriber to handle a specific event type. Subscribers are stored by generic type for type-safe dispatch.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Events.EventPublisher.PublishAsync``1(``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publishes an event to all registered subscribers. Executes synchronously in order of subscription. Catches and logs exceptions from subscribers to prevent cascade failures.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Events.IDomainEvent
member.summary: Base interface for domain events published throughout the authorization server. Enables decoupled communication between different parts of the system. Examples: token issued, user authenticated, consent granted.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Events.IDomainEvent.EventId
member.summary: Unique identifier for this specific event instance. Useful for deduplication and tracking.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Events.IDomainEvent.OccurredAt
member.summary: Timestamp when the event occurred (UTC).

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Events.IDomainEvent.RequestId
member.summary: Request ID for correlation with HTTP request that triggered this event. Helps with request tracing across logs.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Events.IDomainEvent.EventType
member.summary: Human-readable name of the event type.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Events.IEventPublisher
member.summary: Interface for publishing domain events to subscribers. Enables loosely-coupled event-driven patterns within the auth server. Implementations can be synchronous or asynchronous depending on use case.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Events.IEventPublisher.PublishAsync``1(``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publishes a domain event to all registered subscribers. Subscribers are notified synchronously in registration order.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Events.IEventSubscriber`1
member.summary: Interface for subscribing to domain events. Implementations receive notifications when matching events are published.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Events.IEventSubscriber`1.HandleAsync(`0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles publication of a domain event. Should complete quickly to avoid blocking event publication.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Events.TokenIssuedEvent.ClientIpAddress
member.summary: Client IP address for audit/security purposes.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Extensions.DateTimeExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for DateTime operations, particularly for JWT and OAuth2 token handling. Ensures consistent Unix timestamp conversion and expiration checking throughout the system.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Extensions.DateTimeExtensions.ToUnixTimestamp(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Converts a DateTime to Unix epoch time (seconds since January 1, 1970 UTC). Standard for JWT tokens and OAuth2 specifications.
member.param: The DateTime to convert. If not UTC, it will be converted to UTC.
member.returns: Unix epoch time in seconds.

member.summary: Creates a DateTime from Unix epoch time (seconds since January 1, 1970 UTC).
member.returns: A DateTime representing the Unix timestamp in UTC.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Extensions.DateTimeExtensions.IsExpired(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Checks if a token/grant has expired based on its expiration time. Includes a small buffer (5 seconds) to avoid race conditions.
member.returns: if the token/grant has expired; otherwise, .

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Extensions.DateTimeExtensions.IsValid(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Checks if a token/grant is still valid (not yet expired).
member.returns: if the token/grant is still valid; otherwise, .

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Extensions.DateTimeExtensions.RemainingSeconds(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Calculates remaining lifetime in seconds until expiration. Returns 0 if already expired.
member.returns: Remaining lifetime in seconds, or 0 if already expired.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Extensions.DateTimeExtensions.AddLifetime(System.DateTime,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds a configured lifetime (in seconds) to the current time. Ensures consistent expiration time calculation across the system.
member.param: The base DateTime to add lifetime to.
Lifetime in seconds to add. Negative values are treated as 0.
member.returns: A new DateTime with the lifetime added.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Extensions.DateTimeExtensions.ToRfc3339String(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Formats a DateTime as an RFC 3339 string for OAuth2/OIDC responses.
member.returns: RFC 3339 formatted string representation of the DateTime in UTC.

By default an in-process is used. Replace it with a database-backed implementation before deploying to production:
services.AddWebAuthn() .AddSingleton<IWebAuthnCredentialStore, MyDbCredentialStore>();
member.returns: The same instance to allow chaining.

member.summary: Completes a device flow by authorizing it after user verification.

member.summary: Denies a device flow authorization.

member.summary: Polls the status of a device flow authorization. Used by the device to check if user has authorized it.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.DeviceFlowHandler.CompleteDeviceFlow(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a completed device flow session.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.DeviceFlowStatus
member.summary: Status of a device flow authorization.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.JwksHandler

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.JwksHandler.GetJwksAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets the current JWKS (JSON Web Key Set). Returns cached result if available, otherwise generates from current signing key.

member.summary: Generates SHA256 thumbprint of key material. Useful for key identification without exposing full key.

member.summary: JSON Web Key Set response per RFC 7517.

member.summary: Single JSON Web Key (JWK) per RFC 7517.

member.summary: Gets the JWKS response and extracts the first key if available.

member.exception: Thrown when is empty or whitespace.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.TokenRevocationHandler
member.summary: Handler for OAuth2 token revocation (RFC 7009). Allows clients to revoke tokens they no longer need. Removes tokens from server-side storage to prevent their use. Important for logout flows and compromised token recovery.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.TokenRevocationHandler.RevokeTokenAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Revokes a token (typically a refresh token) by removing it from storage. Returns success regardless of token existence to prevent information disclosure. Per RFC 7009, servers MUST respond with 200 OK even for invalid tokens.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.TokenRevocationHandler.RevokeUserTokensAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Revokes all tokens issued to a specific user (logout operation). Used when user password changes or account is compromised.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.TokenRevocationHandler.TryExtractJti(System.String,System.String@,System.DateTime@)
member.summary: Attempts to parse a JWT and extract its jti and expiry without full signature validation. Used to blacklist individual access tokens on revocation.

member.summary: Result of a token revocation operation.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.RevocationResult.Success
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the operation completed without errors. Per RFC 7009, always true if no authentication error occurred.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.RevocationResult.Revoked
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the token was actually found and revoked.

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Handlers.RevocationResult.Error
member.summary: Gets or sets the error message if operation failed.

member.returns: A JSON string representation of the handler.

member.summary: Middleware for establishing request context including request IDs for tracing and correlation. Enables end-to-end request tracking through distributed systems and makes debugging production issues significantly easier.

member.summary: Thread-safe storage for request-scoped logical context data. Used throughout the request pipeline to maintain consistency.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Security.LoginRateLimiter
member.summary: Tracks failed login attempts per username and per IP address using a sliding-window counter and temporarily blocks callers that exceed the configured threshold. This mitigates brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks on the password grant endpoint.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Security.LoginRateLimiter.ThrowIfBlocked(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws (429) if the username or IP is currently blocked due to too many recent failures.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Security.LoginRateLimiter.RecordFailure(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Records a failed login attempt for the username and/or IP.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Security.LoginRateLimiter.RecordSuccess(System.String)
member.summary: Clears the failed-attempt counter for a username after a successful login.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Security.RevokedTokenStore
member.summary: In-memory store for revoked JWT IDs (jti claims). Enables individual access token revocation without invalidating all tokens for a user. Entries are self-expiring: once the original token's expiry passes, the entry is pruned on the next access so the store stays bounded.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Security.RevokedTokenStore.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Security.RevokedTokenStore.Revoke(System.String,System.DateTime)
member.summary: Adds a jti to the revocation list. is used to automatically expire the entry.
member.param: The UTC expiry time of the original token.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Security.RevokedTokenStore.IsRevoked(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true if the given jti has been explicitly revoked and has not yet expired.
member.returns: True if the token is revoked and not expired; otherwise false.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Security.RevokedTokenStore.PurgeExpired
member.summary: Removes all entries whose original tokens have already expired. Called periodically by .

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Security.RevokedTokenStore.RemoveExpired(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Removes all entries whose original tokens have already expired.
member.param: The current UTC time to use for comparison.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Security.RevokedTokenStore.Count
member.summary: Gets the current number of revoked tokens in the store.
member.returns: The count of revoked tokens.

member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum password length. Default: 100

member.summary: Gets or sets whether to require at least one uppercase letter. Default: false

member.summary: Gets or sets whether to require at least one digit. Default: false

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Services.PasswordPolicyOptions.RequireSpecialChar
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to require at least one special character. Default: false

member.summary: Gets or sets whether to prevent password from being equal to username. Default: true

member.name: P:DotnetAuthServer.Services.PasswordPolicyOptions.CheckUsernameVariations
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to check common username variations (username123, 123username, etc.). Default: true

member.summary: Gets or sets whether to check against common patterns (e.g., "password", "123456"). Default: true

member.summary: Gets or sets whether to check against password history. Default: false

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Services.PolicyEnforcementService
member.summary: Service for enforcing RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) and ABAC (Attribute-Based Access Control). Evaluates policies to determine if a user is allowed to perform an action or access a resource. When OPA integration is enabled ( = true) policy decisions are delegated to the Open Policy Agent REST API; otherwise the built-in evaluator is used.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Services.TokenIssuer.HandleRefreshTokenGrantAsync(DotnetAuthServer.Domain.Models.TokenRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles refresh token grant with proper token rotation. The old token is revoked *before* the new token is created so that a concurrent replay of the same token is rejected rather than silently succeeding. Clock-skew tolerance is applied when evaluating expiry so that clients whose clocks lag the server by up to are still served.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Services.TotpService
member.summary: Implements TOTP (RFC 6238) multi-factor authentication. Supports enrollment, verification with a configurable time-step window, and single-use backup code redemption. No external dependencies are required — HMAC-SHA1 is provided by the BCL.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Services.TotpService.#ctor(DotnetAuthServer.Data.Repositories.ITotpCredentialRepository,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{DotnetAuthServer.Services.TotpService},DotnetAuthServer.Configuration.AuthServerOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Services.TotpService.InitiateSetupAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Begins TOTP enrollment for a user. Generates a new secret, provisioning URI and backup codes, persists a pending (unconfirmed) credential, and returns the data needed to present a QR code to the user.
member.param: Username shown in the authenticator app label.
member.returns: Setup payload including the provisioning URI and backup codes.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Services.TotpService.ConfirmSetupAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Confirms TOTP enrollment by verifying the code entered by the user. The credential is enabled only when the code is valid.
member.exception: Thrown when no pending credential exists or the code is invalid.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Services.TotpService.VerifyAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Verifies a TOTP code or a single-use backup code for an already-enabled MFA credential. Backup codes are consumed on use.
member.returns: True if the code is valid and MFA passes; false otherwise.

member.name: M:DotnetAuthServer.Services.TotpService.GetStatusAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the MFA status for a user.

member.summary: Disables and removes the TOTP credential for a user.

member.summary: Verifies a TOTP code against the shared secret using a ±1 step window.

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Services.WebAuthnService
member.summary: Implements WebAuthn Level 2 registration and authentication ceremonies with support for ES256 (ECDSA/P-256, COSE -7) and RS256 (RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5, COSE -257) algorithms. Challenge lifecycle is managed through .

member.summary: Initializes a new instance.

member.summary: Serialises two byte arrays with 4-byte big-endian length prefixes for .

member.name: T:DotnetAuthServer.Services.WebAuthnService.Cbor
member.summary: Minimal CBOR (RFC 7049) reader covering the subset used by WebAuthn attestation objects and COSE_Key structures: definite-length maps, arrays, byte strings, text strings, unsigned/negative integers, and tagged items.
